case_name: Gregory Sage v. James R. Howard, M. D.

summary.brief: Gregory Sage v. James R. Howard, M. D.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Gregory Sage, at the age of thirty-nine, had surgery to replace his left hip in March of 2006.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Following the hip replacement surgery, he experienced dislocation of the hip on several occasions.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Sage could not recall all of the dislocations, but he remembered three occasions when his hip became dislocated while he was simply standing or sitting.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On October 2, 2007, approximately five weeks after surgery, Sage woke up with pain in his left hip.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The nurse who performed the initial assessment of Sage noted that he was in severe pain, had tremors in his left leg, and could not stand.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Howard evaluated and treated Sage in the emergency room.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Howard’s differential diagnosis was fracture or hip dislocation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Dr. Howard did not call Sage’s treating physician, Dr. Heydemann, nor did he consult the on-call orthopedic specialist before attempting to reduce the dislocation.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: He ordered the x-rays of Sage’s left hip and pelvis at 8:17 p.m.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Sage signed the consent form authorizing anesthesia and Propofol was administered at 9:15 p.m.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: A CT scan done after the procedure showed the same fracture seen in the x-rays.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We reverse the summary judgment and remand the cause for further proceedings consistent with this opinion.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient woke up with pain in his left hip, which became more severe throughout the day. He felt a 'pop' when moving across his bed, similar to previous hip dislocations.

focused_exam.hip: Severe pain, tremors in left leg, tenderness to touch, unable to range hip

requestables.name: X-ray of left hip and pelvis
requestables.description: X-ray to assess for dislocation or fracture

reveal.result_summary: Fracture of the upper medial aspect of the femur, total left hip prosthesis in place
reveal.decision_impact: Should have prompted orthopedic consultation before reduction

requestables.name: CT scan of left hip
requestables.description: CT scan to confirm findings of X-ray

reveal.clinical_significance: Reinforces the presence of a fracture

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with acute left hip pain

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with suspected hip dislocation or fracture
decision_points.prompt: What should be done after obtaining X-ray results?

options.description: Consult orthopedic specialist before attempting reduction
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care requires specialist input for complex cases

options.description: Proceed with hip reduction without consultation
options.clinical_reasoning: Risk of worsening fracture without specialist input

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant proceeded with reduction without consultation
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Believed reduction was necessary based on history

expected_action_court.description: Orthopedic consultation should have been obtained
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated need for specialist input

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to consult increased risk of fracture worsening
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Orthopedic consultation and possible surgical intervention
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient required surgical repair of fractured femur
patient_outcome.long_term_effects: Potential for chronic pain and mobility issues

key_findings: Summary judgment reversed and remanded for further proceedings

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Consultation could have prevented fracture worsening

tests_performed.test_name: X-ray of left hip and pelvis
tests_performed.result: Fracture of the upper medial aspect of the femur
